meta.testbed_note: 78738 = Bee Cave / Lakeway / Lake Travis, Travis County, TX. Affluent but low rooftop-density Hill Country in the Austin MSA, served by the ERCOT grid. Good stress test for "why no Costco/Chipotle yet" (it's drive-time trade-area and rooftop counts, not income) and for data-center siting (ERCOT power + water scarcity + flood/terrain constraints).

census_acs5.name: American Community Survey 5-Year Detailed Tables
census_acs5.relevance: The core demand signal. Costco/Chipotle siting models run on rooftops and households within a drive-time ring, plus income and household size — all here at block-group resolution.

census_tiger.name: TIGER/Line Shapefiles & TIGERweb
census_tiger.relevance: Provides the geometry (ZCTA 78738, tracts, county) we join every other dataset onto.

census_cbp_zbp.name: County / ZIP Business Patterns (CBP / ZBP)
census_cbp_zbp.relevance: Counts of establishments by industry (NAICS) per ZIP — tells us how saturated grocery/warehouse-club/fast-casual already is around 78738.

bls_qcew.name: Quarterly Census of Employment & Wages
bls_qcew.relevance: Daytime employment & wage base — matters for lunch-traffic businesses (Chipotle) more than residential population alone.

lehd_lodes.name: LEHD Origin-Destination Employment Statistics (LODES)
lehd_lodes.relevance: Where 78738 residents actually work (mostly into Austin) and who commutes in — drives the "trade area is bigger than the rooftops" insight.

osm_overpass.name: OpenStreetMap (Overpass API)
osm_overpass.relevance: The single most useful free POI layer. Lets us locate the *existing* Costco (Cedar Park / Southwest Austin), Chipotles, grocery stores, schools, fire stations, and the road network — all in one queryable source.

nominatim.name: Nominatim Geocoding
nominatim.relevance: Turns a street address or parcel into a coordinate to anchor analysis.

openrouteservice.name: openrouteservice (isochrones & routing)
openrouteservice.relevance: THE key to retail siting. A Costco needs ~X households inside a 10-15 min drive. Hill Country roads (few arterials, lake barriers) make 78738's drive-time trade area much smaller than its radius suggests — likely the real reason there's no warehouse club yet.

ntad_roads.name: National Transportation Atlas Database (NTAD)
ntad_roads.relevance: Traffic counts (AADT) on TX-71 / RR 620 / Bee Cave Rd — retailers care a lot about cars-past-the-door.

eia_opendata.name: EIA Open Data API v2
eia_opendata.relevance: Generation inventory & retail electricity price — first-order input for the data-center example (is there spare generation in the region, what does power cost).

hifld_power.name: HIFLD Open — Energy Infrastructure (plants, substations, transmission)
hifld_power.relevance: Locates the nearest substations & transmission lines. A data center can't just "plug in" — proximity to high-voltage capacity is decisive and this is the geospatial layer for it.

ercot_grid.name: ERCOT Public Reports & Interconnection Queue
ercot_grid.relevance: 78738 is inside ERCOT. The interconnection queue shows where large new loads (data centers) and generators are already lining up — directly answers "can the grid here absorb a 100MW data center, and at what scale of upgrade." Texas-specific superpower for this testbed.

nrel_resource.name: NREL Renewable Resource & Utility Rate APIs
nrel_resource.relevance: Solar resource & local utility rate (which utility serves the parcel, $/kWh).

epa_sdwis_echo.name: EPA SDWIS / ECHO (public water systems & discharges)
epa_sdwis_echo.relevance: Which water system serves the parcel and its capacity/population served. Critical for water-hungry uses (data-center cooling, manufacturing) in a drought-prone Hill Country region.

usgs_nhd_water.name: USGS National Hydrography Dataset & Water Services
usgs_nhd_water.relevance: Lake Travis & Colorado River proximity, watershed boundaries, and gauge flows — supply context and a hard constraint for high-water uses.

fcc_bdc.name: FCC National Broadband Map (Broadband Data Collection)
fcc_bdc.relevance: What internet (fiber? cable? fixed wireless?) actually reaches the parcel. A data center / office needs redundant high-capacity fiber; large parts of the Hill Country are still cable/DSL/fixed-wireless only.

nces_schools.name: NCES / EDGE — Schools & Enrollment
nces_schools.relevance: Lake Travis ISD & Eanes ISD capacity. New rooftops trigger school demand; the dependency engine uses enrollment headroom to flag when a development "needs a school."

hifld_civic.name: HIFLD Open — Public Safety & Health Facilities
hifld_civic.relevance: Nearest fire station / ESD coverage, hospital beds, response-time gaps. Feeds the "at what scale does this development require a new fire station" threshold logic.

cms_hospitals.name: CMS / HRSA Health Facility Data
cms_hospitals.relevance: Hospital capacity & service lines within the drive-time ring.

tcad_parcels.name: Travis County Parcels & Land Use (TNR GeoHub / TCAD)
tcad_parcels.relevance: The parcel layer for the testbed — "if I buy THIS plot." Gives boundary, acreage, current land use and valuation. National parcel coverage is fragmented/county-by-county, so we model the county-portal pattern here and generalize the connector later.

overture_buildings.name: Overture Maps / Microsoft Building Footprints
overture_buildings.relevance: Actual rooftop count & built density — the truest proxy for "how many customers physically live here." In low-density Hill Country this number is the crux of the Costco question.

fema_nfhl.name: FEMA National Flood Hazard Layer
fema_nfhl.relevance: Hill Country = flash-flood country (Flash Flood Alley). Floodway/100-yr zones are a hard developability constraint on any parcel near a creek or the lake.

usgs_3dep.name: USGS 3DEP Elevation / The National Map
usgs_3dep.relevance: Slope/terrain drives grading cost. Steep Hill Country lots can be far more expensive to develop than flat ones of the same price.

usfws_critical_habitat.name: USFWS Critical Habitat & Endangered Species (ECOS)
usfws_critical_habitat.relevance: The Hill Country is designated habitat for the endangered golden-cheeked warbler (and historically black-capped vireo). Clearing oak-juniper for a subdivision or data center can trigger Endangered Species Act review — a first-order "should we" constraint, not just a cost.

padus_protected_areas.name: PAD-US Protected Areas Database
padus_protected_areas.relevance: The Balcones Canyonlands Preserve sits in/around 78738 — large tracts are already off-limits by conservation agreement. Defines what is permanently undevelopable and what abuts sensitive land.

nlcd_landcover.name: NLCD Land Cover, Impervious Surface & Tree Canopy
nlcd_landcover.relevance: Quantifies what's there now (oak-juniper woodland vs. cleared) and the impervious-cover increase a development would add — central to both carbon (canopy loss) and water-quality impact over the aquifer.

gbif_biodiversity.name: GBIF Species Occurrence
gbif_biodiversity.relevance: Crowdsourced species observations near a parcel — a biodiversity signal that complements the regulatory habitat layers (what actually lives here, not just what's legally designated).

tceq_edwards_aquifer.name: Edwards Aquifer Recharge/Contributing Zones (TCEQ)
tceq_edwards_aquifer.relevance: Parts of the area drain to the Edwards Aquifer. Development over the recharge/contributing zone faces strict impervious-cover and water-quality rules (TCEQ Edwards Rules) — a hard, locally famous "should/can we" gate.

demand.label: Demand / Customers
demand.provides: rooftops, households, daytime population, spending power

entitlement.label: Zoning / Entitlement
entitlement.provides: legal permission to build a use at a density

absorption.label: Market Absorption (demand-to-build)
absorption.provides: confidence that built homes/space will sell or lease quickly

habitat.label: Habitat & Biodiversity
habitat.provides: ecological value at risk from development

land_cover.label: Land Cover & Impervious Surface
land_cover.provides: natural land lost and runoff added by development

carbon.label: Carbon
carbon.provides: emissions from construction, operation, and canopy/soil loss

warehouse_club.label: Warehouse Club (Costco-type)

transportation.note: major traffic generator; may require signal/turn-lane upgrades

warehouse_club.notes: Costco's own rule of thumb is a large rooftop base within a short drive and a big flat pad. 78738 likely fails the drive-time household count, not income.

fast_casual.label: Fast-Casual Restaurant (Chipotle-type)
fast_casual.notes: Drafts off daytime/lunch population and car traffic more than nighttime rooftops — hence why an office node or retail anchor matters.

data_center.label: Data Center

water.note: for evaporative cooling; air-cooled relaxes this

safety.trigger: hazmat/electrical load may raise ESD/fire requirements

power.trigger: >50MW load typically forces transmission upgrade / ERCOT interconnection study

data_center.notes: The flagship dependency example. Power + water + fiber are the gating trio; ERCOT interconnection queue position determines real-world timeline. Its high carbon + water impacts make it the sharpest "should we?" case.

residential_subdivision.label: Residential Subdivision (housing-developer lens)

amenities.signal: retail / schools / services already present nearby

price.signal: achievable price > land + infra + construction cost

transportation.note: trip generation may require road widening

residential_subdivision.notes: The canonical SimCity feedback loop AND the chicken-and-egg crux: rooftops create the demand that makes retail viable — but developers build rooftops only when demand signals (jobs, amenities, absorption) are already present. When the amenity they're waiting for is itself waiting for rooftops, you get a standoff. See `enabling_edges` below and `simy standoffs`.

employment_center.label: Employment Center (office / industrial jobs)
employment_center.note: A jobs anchor. Often the public-incentive lever that breaks a housing/retail standoff.

broadband_buildout.label: Broadband Buildout (ISP investment)
broadband_buildout.note: Private ISP capital expansion; gated by rooftop density, so itself part of standoffs.

enabling_edges.via: rooftops / household count in trade area
retail amenity raises home absorption & price
redundant fiber is a hard data-center gate
rooftop density makes ISP buildout profitable

developer.label: Developer
developer.question: Will this make money?
developer.note: Structurally pro-build; the real leaning is gated by market/absorption data (M1).

resident.label: Existing Resident
resident.question: Does this improve daily life without ruining the area?
resident.note: Wants amenities and services nearby, but not the traffic and sprawl that come with them.

environmentalist.label: Environmentalist
environmentalist.question: What does this destroy or emit?
environmentalist.note: Weighs habitat loss, impervious cover, carbon, and water stress against any built benefit.

municipality.label: Municipality / Taxpayer
municipality.question: Does the tax base cover the public services it triggers?
municipality.note: Welcomes tax base but bears the cost of every induced school, fire station, and road.
